Add 6px dead zone before element move commits

Prevents accidental element displacement during select/tap, especially
with pen and touch input where small pointer movements are common.
The dead zone is measured in screen pixels (zoom-independent). Undo is
deferred until the move actually commits, keeping the undo stack clean
for tap-to-select interactions.

[Repo Assist] Fix `open` insertion position for nested explicit modules (issue #789) (#1495)

* Fix open insertion to place inside nearest enclosing module (issue #789)

FCS's TryFindInsertionContext sets Pos.Line to the module *declaration*
line (e.g. 'module Foo =') rather than the first line inside the module.
adjustInsertionPoint passed this value straight through for ScopeKind.Module,
which caused the generated 'open' to be inserted *before* the module
declaration instead of inside it.

Fix: detect when the candidate insertion line is an explicit module
declaration ('module X =') and advance by one so the open lands inside
the module.  This makes the code-fix behaviour consistent with code
completion (issue #789).

Also adds isExplicitModuleDeclaration helper and a regression test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v0.4.1: Fix Wi-Fi hotspot on Pi Zero 2 W

Root causes diagnosed from USB gadget + prior image inspection:

1. rfkill unblock ran at BUILD TIME in chroot — completely ineffective.
   rfkill state resets on every boot. Fix: logfalcon-wifi-init.service
   runs 'rfkill unblock all' as a oneshot before hostapd.

2. Regulatory domain never set at runtime. country_code=US in hostapd.conf
   is not enough — cfg80211 (the Linux wireless subsystem) must have the
   country set at the kernel level before hostapd can request AP mode.
   On Pi Zero 2 W this blocks AP startup entirely. Fix: wifi-init service
   runs 'iw reg set US' before hostapd; also write minimal
   wpa_supplicant.conf with country=US so cfg80211 picks it up from file.

3. wpa_supplicant@wlan0.service (per-interface instance unit used by Pi
   Zero 2 W Bookworm) was not disabled — only the main unit was. Fix:
   remove both instance and main unit symlinks, and mask both via /dev/null
   so they cannot be activated by D-Bus or dependency pulls.

4. usb0 (USB gadget ethernet) now gets a static IP (10.55.55.1) via
   dhcpcd so SSH debugging over USB OTG works out of the box.
   Connect the data USB port to Mac, set Mac to 10.55.55.2, ssh pi@10.55.55.1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix rendering efficiency and screencopy Y-flip

Rendering: Skip the full bind/clear/draw/finish/submit cycle when
nothing visual has changed (no new Electron frames, no dirty Wayland
surfaces, no cursor movement). In the DRM path, also skip when no
screencopy or portal streams are active. Frame callbacks are always
sent unconditionally to prevent Wayland client stalls.

Screencopy: Remove the incorrect row-flip after glReadPixels in both
the wlr-screencopy fulfillment and portal stream readback paths.
Smithay already applies a Y-correction when rendering to GBM-backed
FBOs, so glReadPixels returns top-down data. The manual flip was
inverting an already-correct image, producing upside-down output.

commit.message:
Add ARM32 (armv7/thumbv7) thread-local dispatch for parallel test execution (#114)

* Add ARM32 (armv7/thumbv7) thread-local dispatch for parallel test support

Implement thread-local dispatch for ARM32 architecture, matching the
existing x86_64 and aarch64 implementations. This enables parallel test
execution on ARM32 Linux targets by replacing the global mutex approach
with per-thread function replacement routing.

Changes:
- Add target_arch = "arm" to all thread-local dispatch cfg gates across
  thread_local_registry.rs, internal.rs, injector.rs, common.rs, and
  thread_safety.rs
- Implement ARM32 dispatcher JIT code generation (ARM mode) with
  register save/restore for both integer (r0-r3) and VFP (d0-d7) regs
- Add ARM32 trampoline creation with support for both ARM and Thumb mode
  jump-back sequences
- Add PC-relative instruction fixup for ARM mode trampolines
- Add ARM32 branch patch generation matching existing patch_arm.rs format
  including Thumb alignment handling
- Add ARM32 JIT code for boolean return values (MOV r0, #imm; BX lr)
- Enable allocate_jit_memory for ARM32 (uses simple mmap fallback)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ARM32 dispatcher stack alignment and patch register clobbering

- Fix AAPCS stack alignment violation: PUSH {r0-r3, r4, lr} (24 bytes,
  8-aligned) instead of PUSH {r0-r3, lr} (20 bytes, misaligned).
  Misaligned SP before BLX caused SIGABRT on armv7.

- Use r12 (IP, scratch register) instead of r7 (callee-saved/frame pointer)
  for Thumb branch patch. r7 clobbering corrupted the caller's frame pointer,
  causing SIGSEGV on thumbv7. Uses MOVW/MOVT r12 encoding which is also
  alignment-independent.

- Use r12 instead of r9 (callee-saved) for ARM branch patch.

* Reduce ARM32 test stress and add diagnostics for CI

- Add RUST_BACKTRACE=full for ARM target CI runs
- Run ARM tests with --test-threads=1 to avoid resource exhaustion
  on 32-bit compat runners (armv7 was hitting thread spawn limits)
- Reduce stress test THREAD_COUNT from 20 to 4 on ARM32

* Fix ARM32 function overflow with dynamic 4-byte patch size

Use dynamic patch sizing for ARM32: 4-byte B.W (Thumb) or B (ARM) when
the dispatcher is within branch range (±16MB/±32MB), falling back to
12-byte MOVW/MOVT/BX when it is not. This eliminates the previous
12-byte patch overflow into adjacent small functions that caused
SIGSEGV on thumbv7.

Key changes:
- Add ARM32 to constrained JIT allocation (±16MB range) in common.rs
- Pre-allocate dispatcher, then choose patch size based on distance
- For Thumb 4-byte patches, prepend a Thumb-to-ARM stub (LDR.W/BX)
  since Thumb B.W cannot switch processor mode
- Encode Thumb B.W (T4) and ARM B (unconditional) for 4-byte patches
- Add -j 1 to CI cargo test for ARM targets to prevent resource
  exhaustion from parallel test binary execution

* Add diagnostics for thumbv7 SIGSEGV in test_will_execute_raw_closure_per_thread

Add eprintln diagnostics to:
- install_dispatcher_arm32: log addresses, distance, patch size
- generate_branch_patch_arm32: verify B.W encoding round-trips correctly
- test_will_execute_raw_closure_per_thread: log each step to identify crash point

Also add isolated test run CI step for ARM targets to determine if the
crash is caused by accumulated state from prior tests.

* Fix ARM32 Thumb overlapping patch SIGILL and instruction boundary bugs

- Use std::hint::black_box in test helper functions to ensure minimum
  4-byte code size on Thumb (prevents compiler from generating 2-byte
  functions that cause overlapping patches)
- Add calculate_thumb_copy_size() to scan Thumb instructions and find
  the first complete instruction boundary >= patch_size bytes, preventing
  the trampoline from splitting a 32-bit Thumb instruction
- Add check_arm32_patch_overlap() to detect overlapping patches at
  runtime with clear diagnostic messages (only checks active patches
  with ref_count > 0)
- Remove diagnostic eprintln statements and isolated test CI step

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: robust Prometheus connection check + node filter support

Connection check fixes:
- check_connection() now falls back to /api/v1/query when /-/healthy
  returns non-200 (previously only fell back on exceptions)
- Add diagnose_connection() returning per-probe results (url,
  status_code, error) for all attempted endpoints
- CLI prints detailed probe table and actionable hints on failure

Node filter feature:
- Add --node / -n and --node-label CLI params (also via TARGET_NODE,
  NODE_LABEL env vars)
- apply_node_filter() injects label selector into bare metrics,
  metrics with existing selectors, and rate()-style PromQL functions
- anomaly_detector and tools.py both apply node filter to all queries
- Startup panel shows target node and label name

Tests: 66 passing (10 new tests for diagnose_connection and
apply_node_filter, including 404-fallback scenario)

commit.message:
refactor(mcp): DRY iOS/Android handlers via macros — 706→363 lines (49%)

Introduce `ios!()` and `droid!()` macros to eliminate the repeated
lock-and-unwrap boilerplate in every MCP handler method.

Before: 3 lines of `self.mobile.lock().await` + `as_ref().ok_or_else()`
After: 1 line `ios!(self, c)` or `droid!(self, c)`

- iOS handlers: 363→176 lines (−51%)
- Android handlers: 343→187 lines (−45%)
- Combined: 706→363 lines (−49%)
- All 572 tests pass, zero compiler warnings, identical API

* Fix missing static web assets in NuGet package (#118)

The Razor SDK generates the static web assets manifest at dotnet build
time. Previously, npm run build (which outputs the JS files to wwwroot/)
ran after dotnet build, so the manifest was generated with an empty
wwwroot/ and dotnet pack --no-build reused that stale manifest.

Fix by moving the npm install/lint/build steps before dotnet restore and
dotnet build, ensuring the compiled JS files exist in wwwroot/ when the
static web assets manifest is generated.

commit.message:
Fix resize: sticky cursor + anchor-based 1:1 pointer tracking

Cursor fix: set ws.style.cursor to the handle's resize cursor at drag
start, matching how pan already sets 'grabbing'. Previously the cursor
reverted to crosshair/default once pointer capture moved events to the
workspace element.

Tracking fix: replace center-based scaling with anchor-based resize.
The opposite corner/edge stays pinned in world space while the dragged
handle follows the pointer 1:1. Math projects pointer deltas into the
element's local (rotation-aware) coordinate frame, so rotated elements
resize correctly. Grab offset prevents initial position jump.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fixes handling of native / alien widgets when (un)docking (#820)

* Initial plan

* Fix drawing artifacts when content widgets call winId()

When a content widget (e.g. VTK/OpenGL) calls winId() to get a native
window handle, Qt propagates native-window creation up the ancestor chain.
This makes CDockWidget, CDockAreaWidget, and CDockContainerWidget all
become native OS windows unexpectedly. Reparenting these native dock
widgets during floating or resizing then causes drawing artifacts.

Fix: set Qt::WA_DontCreateNativeAncestors on the content widget in
CDockWidget::setWidget() to stop native-window creation from propagating
up into the dock hierarchy.

Co-authored-by: RubendeBruin <34062862+RubendeBruin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix docking system to handle mix of native and alien Qt widgets

Three targeted fixes for the native/alien widget mixing problem:

1. Revert WA_DontCreateNativeAncestors from CDockWidget::setWidget().
   Setting it on the content widget created a broken native-inside-alien
   hierarchy: the QScrollArea (alien) could not clip its native child, so
   content escaped the scrollbox bounds; and alien CAutoHideDockContainer
   could not appear above the native content widget.
   Allowing natural propagation means QScrollArea also goes native, and OS
   parent/child window clipping works correctly.

2. Fix CDockContainerWidget::removeDockArea to use internalWinId() instead
   of testAttribute(WA_NativeWindow). WA_NativeWindow is only set when a
   widget is explicitly made native; widgets that became native through
   upward propagation from a descendant calling winId() have a real native
   window handle (internalWinId() != 0) but WA_NativeWindow may be unset.
   Setting their parent to nullptr would create a free-floating invisible
   OS window and cause drawing artifacts.

3. Fix CAutoHideDockContainer::collapseView: when the parent dock container
   is native but the auto-hide panel is still alien, promote the panel to a
   native window (winId()) before raise(). The OS always renders native
   child windows above a parent window's painted alien content, so an alien
   auto-hide panel can never appear above native sibling dock areas. Once
   the panel has its own OS-level window, raise() operates on OS Z-order
   and correctly places it above native content siblings.

Co-authored-by: RubendeBruin <34062862+RubendeBruin@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix RPC starvation on 1-CPU VPS during block sync

Root cause: tokio defaults to num_cpus worker threads (1 on VPS).
Synchronous sled I/O during block processing monopolizes the only
worker thread, starving RPC, timers, and network I/O indefinitely.

Three fixes:
- Force worker_threads=4 in tokio::main regardless of CPU count
- Replace yield_now() with sleep(1ms) between block batches so the
  I/O driver gets polled (yield_now re-queues without polling I/O)
- Add flush_storage_async() using spawn_blocking for fsync calls
  so they run on the blocking thread pool, not tokio workers

feat: per-environment platform selection (--claude/--copilot)

Each environment now targets exactly one platform:

- skenv create myenv --copilot (-> ~/.copilot/skills/)

- Freeze manifests include platform, init reads it

- Base layer remains universal (synced to whichever platform is active)

commit.message:
Make hooks the ONLY DB writer — remove agent manual INSERT

Root cause: agents manually INSERT into tasks table with guessed
project_id, causing misattribution. Hooks have a deterministic
registration gate (cwd → folder match) but agents don't.

Fix:
- Removed 'insert a row into tasks table' from copilot-instructions.md
  and CLAUDE.md. Agents never touch progress.db directly.
- Rewrote progress-logger.sh to read Copilot CLI session state:
  workspace.yaml (summary) + checkpoints (overview, takeaways, gotchas)
  instead of just file diffs.
- Falls back to activity log file changes if no session state found.
- Skips index.md when finding latest checkpoint.

The hook pipeline is now the single deterministic write path:
  sessionEnd → match cwd to project → read session state → INSERT

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
